title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The company will hire more staff if sales increase.
‘Don't forget to take your keys,’ she said to him.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with object + to-infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: The only way he could pass was by cheating.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for past impossibility.
questions.secondSentence: If he hadn't cheated, he (1) _________ to pass.

questions.firstSentence: I am certain that the team practiced very hard.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for positive past deduction.

questions.firstSentence: As soon as I sat down, the phone rang.
questions.secondSentence: No (1) _________ down than the phone rang.

questions.firstSentence: I don't think I can ever get used to this cold weather.
questions.secondSentence: I don't think I can ever (1) _________ this cold weather.
